_comment: Wonderbly 'Father's Day book swap' flat-lay product reveal — the worked example. Copy to config.json and edit. Each beat: a flat top-down product shot on a dressed tabletop, hands cup it + lift it away. 1080x1920 9:16. Two paid model calls PER BEAT (flat cover + Veo i2v) + optional FD-card gen + music.

flat_cover.prompt: Top-down overhead photograph of this exact product/cover lying perfectly flat on a clean white background. True 90-degree top-down camera angle, the front face fills the frame as a perfect square (1:1). Natural hardcover thickness as a thin edge. The cover art, illustration, title text, personalized names, decorative elements, and logo must remain IDENTICAL to the reference — do NOT alter, redesign, relayout, restyle, recolor, or change typography or names. Only change the camera angle from 3D-perspective to true top-down flat square. Soft natural daylight upper-left, subtle drop-shadow under the item. No other props, plain white surface.

beat_i2v.prompt: Two adult human hands ENTER from the bottom corners and CUP the item centered on the tabletop. OPENING (0-0.5s): the item is already centered — DO NOT MOVE IT, DO NOT CHANGE THE COVER ART/TEXT/NAMES/LOGO from the start frame. PRESENTATION (0.5-2.5s): both hands enter bottom-left and bottom-right, palms down, cup the bottom edge, HOLD the item centered ~1.5s, cover fully faces camera, all text readable. EXIT (2.5-3.7s): hands LIFT the item 2cm and CARRY it off the bottom edge, fully exiting by 3.7s. CLOSE (3.7-4s): tabletop empty. STYLE: top-down 90-degree overhead, camera does NOT move, wide depth of field, soft daylight upper-left, real linen texture + real skin micro-detail. ABSOLUTE: the item's art/text/names/logo must be PRESERVED EXACTLY from the start frame — no smear, no redesign, no substitution.

insert_card.illustration_prompt: A warm Father's Day greeting-card illustration in the brand's soft storybook style, no text, cream background, centered motif (e.g. dad + child, hearts, sun).

music.prompt: Lively warm acoustic Father's Day bed, ~130 BPM, festive and heartfelt but NOT jingle-bell cliché, light guitar + soft percussion + warm bass, major key, instrumental only, energetic from t=0.
